title: SigmaBlockedWalks
tex_block_html:
Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $C \ins J \cup V$ a subset of nodes and $\pi$ a walk in $G$: \[ \pi =\lp v_0 \sus \cdots \sus v_n \rp. \] We say that the walk $\pi$ is:

  1. $C$-$\sigma$-open (or $\sigma$-open given $C$) if and only if:
    1. all colliders $v_k$ on $\pi$ are in $\Anc^G(C)$, and:
    2. all blockable non-colliders $v_k$ on $\pi$ are not in $C$.
  2. $C$-$\sigma$-blocked (or $\sigma$-blocked given $C$) if and only if:
    1. there exists a collider $v_k$ on $\pi$ that is not in $\Anc^G(C)$, or:
    2. there exists a blockable non-collider $v_k$ on $\pi$ in $C$.

Note that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \ins V \cup J$.

tex_statement.raw:
% Definition file for row def_3_17 -- "SigmaBlockedWalks".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solve_chapter.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker dispatched by it) fills the body below with the
% Lean-faithful definition statement(s). Stays close to the lecture notes.
%
% This file is a sibling of `main.tex` inside `<subsection>/tex/`. The
% `subfiles` package lets it compile both standalone (resolving its
% preamble via `main.tex`) and as part of `main.tex` via `\subfile{...}`.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: def_3_17
% title: SigmaBlockedWalks
\def\rowref{def\_3\_17}
\phantomsection\label{def_3_17}

\begin{Def}[$\sigma$-blocked walks]\label{def:sigma_blocking}
    Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def \ref{def-cdmg}); throughout we use the notation of def \ref{not-cdmg}, the family-relationships definition of def \ref{def_3_5}, the collider/non-collider machinery of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, and the blockable/unblockable refinement of def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}. Let $C \subseteq J \cup V$ be a subset of nodes, and let
    \[
        \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n)
    \]
    be a walk in $G$ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.) for some integer $n \ge 0$, with $v_0, \dots, v_n \in J \cup V$ and $a_0, \dots, a_{n-1} \in E \cup L$ satisfying the walk constraint at every $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$:
    \[
        \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \;\land\; a_k \in E \cup L\bigr)
        \;\lor\;
        \bigl(a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \;\land\; a_k \in E\bigr).
    \]

    \emph{Ancestor set of $C$.}
    The \emph{ancestor set} $\Anc^G(C) \subseteq J \cup V$ is the indexed union of the per-vertex ancestor sets of def \ref{def_3_5}, item~iv.:
    \[
        \Anc^G(C) := \bigcup_{c \in C} \Anc^G(c).
    \]
    Equivalently, for any $w \in J \cup V$,
    \[
        w \in \Anc^G(C) \quad\iff\quad \exists\, c \in C \text{ such that } w \in \Anc^G(c).
    \]
    In the clauses below we use $\Anc^G(C)$ in this union sense.

    \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-open walk.}
    The walk $\pi$ is said to be \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-open} (also called \emph{$\sigma$-open given $C$}) iff both of the following universal conditions hold:
    \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
        \item for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.\ (equivalently, $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$), then $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$;
        \item for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider} (paragraph ``Blockable non-collider on $\pi$''), then $v_k \notin C$.
    \end{enumerate}
    Both clauses are universally quantified over the position index set $\{0, 1, \dots, n\}$; non-collider positions that are \emph{unblockable} (def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}, paragraph ``Unblockable non-collider on $\pi$'') are not constrained by clause~ii., and collider positions are not constrained by clause~ii.\ either (clause~i.\ handles them and clause~ii.\ is vacuous on collider positions since ``blockable non-collider'' carries ``non-collider'' as part of its precondition per def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}).

    \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-blocked walk.}
    The walk $\pi$ is said to be \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-blocked} (also called \emph{$\sigma$-blocked given $C$}) iff at least one of the following existential conditions holds:
    \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
        \item there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ (def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.) and $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$;
        \item there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ (def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}, paragraph ``Blockable non-collider on $\pi$'') and $v_k \in C$.
    \end{enumerate}

    \emph{De Morgan duality of the two classifications.}
    Inspecting the two clause-pairs above, clause~i.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-blocked'' is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause~i.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' (``there exists a collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$'' is the negation of ``every collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$''), and clause~ii.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-blocked'' is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause~ii.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' (``there exists a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \in C$'' is the negation of ``every blockable non-collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \notin C$''). Disjoining the two negated clauses thus gives the negation of the conjunction of the two original clauses; consequently
    \[
        \pi \text{ is } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-blocked} \quad\iff\quad \pi \text{ is not } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},
    \]
    and the two notions are by construction mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive over walks in $G$.

    \emph{Treatment of the trailing LN remark.}
    The LN source block (\texttt{graphs.tex}, \texttt{\textbackslash label\{def:sigma\_blocking\}}) follows the two definitions above with an embedded \texttt{claimmark} reading ``Note that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \subseteq J \cup V$.'' Per the row's authoritative \texttt{addition\_to\_the\_LN} clause \texttt{[claim\_type\_mismatch\_vertex\_vs\_walk]}, that remark is \emph{intentionally excluded} from the formalization of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}: it is informal commentary, not part of the definition proper, and it applies the predicate ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' to \emph{vertices} (specifically, to unblockable non-collider occurrences on a walk), whereas the formal definition above only ever introduces ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' as a property of \emph{walks}. No Lean obligation is to be derived from the embedded remark within this definition. Any walk-level reformulation of its underlying content is handled separately as the dedicated claim row \texttt{claim\_3\_21}; the present definition treats the embedded remark as out of scope.
\end{Def}

\end{document}
tex_statement.html:
Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def def-cdmg); throughout we use the notation of def not-cdmg, the family-relationships definition of def def_3_5, the collider/non-collider machinery of def def:collider_noncollider, and the blockable/unblockable refinement of def def:unblockable_noncollider. Let $C \subseteq J \cup V$ be a subset of nodes, and let \[ \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n) \] be a walk in $G$ (def def:walks, item i.) for some integer $n \ge 0$, with $v_0, \dots, v_n \in J \cup V$ and $a_0, \dots, a_{n-1} \in E \cup L$ satisfying the walk constraint at every $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$: \[ \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \;\land\; a_k \in E \cup L\bigr) \;\lor\; \bigl(a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \;\land\; a_k \in E\bigr). \]

Ancestor set of $C$. The ancestor set $\Anc^G(C) \subseteq J \cup V$ is the indexed union of the per-vertex ancestor sets of def def_3_5, item iv.: \[ \Anc^G(C) := \bigcup_{c \in C} \Anc^G(c). \] Equivalently, for any $w \in J \cup V$, \[ w \in \Anc^G(C) \quad\iff\quad \exists\, c \in C \text{ such that } w \in \Anc^G(c). \] In the clauses below we use $\Anc^G(C)$ in this union sense.

$C$-$\sigma$-open walk. The walk $\pi$ is said to be $C$-$\sigma$-open (also called $\sigma$-open given $C$) iff both of the following universal conditions hold:

  1. for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def def:collider_noncollider, item ii.\ (equivalently, $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$), then $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$;
  2. for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def def:unblockable_noncollider (paragraph “Blockable non-collider on $\pi$”), then $v_k \notin C$.

Both clauses are universally quantified over the position index set $\{0, 1, \dots, n\}$; non-collider positions that are unblockable (def def:unblockable_noncollider, paragraph “Unblockable non-collider on $\pi$”) are not constrained by clause ii., and collider positions are not constrained by clause ii.\ either (clause i.\ handles them and clause ii.\ is vacuous on collider positions since “blockable non-collider” carries “non-collider” as part of its precondition per def def:unblockable_noncollider).

$C$-$\sigma$-blocked walk. The walk $\pi$ is said to be $C$-$\sigma$-blocked (also called $\sigma$-blocked given $C$) iff at least one of the following existential conditions holds:

  1. there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ (def def:collider_noncollider, item ii.) and $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$;
  2. there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ (def def:unblockable_noncollider, paragraph “Blockable non-collider on $\pi$”) and $v_k \in C$.

De Morgan duality of the two classifications. Inspecting the two clause-pairs above, clause i.\ of “$C$-$\sigma$-blocked” is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause i.\ of “$C$-$\sigma$-open” (“there exists a collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$” is the negation of “every collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$”), and clause ii.\ of “$C$-$\sigma$-blocked” is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause ii.\ of “$C$-$\sigma$-open” (“there exists a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \in C$” is the negation of “every blockable non-collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \notin C$”). Disjoining the two negated clauses thus gives the negation of the conjunction of the two original clauses; consequently \[ \pi \text{ is } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-blocked} \quad\iff\quad \pi \text{ is not } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}, \] and the two notions are by construction mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive over walks in $G$.

Treatment of the trailing LN remark. The LN source block (graphs.tex, \texttt{\label\{def:sigma\_blocking\}}) follows the two definitions above with an embedded claimmark reading “Note that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \subseteq J \cup V$.” Per the row's authoritative addition_to_the_LN clause [claim_type_mismatch_vertex_vs_walk], that remark is intentionally excluded from the formalization of def def:sigma_blocking: it is informal commentary, not part of the definition proper, and it applies the predicate “$C$-$\sigma$-open” to vertices (specifically, to unblockable non-collider occurrences on a walk), whereas the formal definition above only ever introduces “$C$-$\sigma$-open” as a property of walks. No Lean obligation is to be derived from the embedded remark within this definition. Any walk-level reformulation of its underlying content is handled separately as the dedicated claim row claim_3_21; the present definition treats the embedded remark as out of scope.

lean_blocks.explanation: This is a `variable` declaration: Lean auto-binds `Node : Type*` and the instance `[DecidableEq Node]` as implicit arguments to every subsequent declaration in the surrounding `CDMG` namespace, sparing us from rewriting the same parameters on `IsSigmaOpenGiven` and `IsSigmaBlockedGiven`. The `Type*` notation makes `Node` universe-polymorphic, so callers can instantiate it at any universe level. The `DecidableEq Node` assumption is inherited from the chapter-wide convention: although the two predicates in this row are propositional and do not themselves run any decision procedure, they reach into `G.AncSet C` and `p.IsCollider k` / `p.IsBlockableNonCollider k`, which in turn unfold through `Finset.filter`, `Finset.image`, and option-valued list lookups on `p.vertices` — all of which need decidable equality on the element type. Stronger structure on `Node` (e.g.\ `Fintype`, `LinearOrder`) is not required here and is deferred to downstream consumers that actually use it. Same convention as the rest of `Chapter3_GraphTheory`'s files.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Auto-binds `Node` + `DecidableEq Node` as implicits; decidability is inherited by `G.AncSet`, `p.IsCollider`, `p.IsBlockableNonCollider` below.
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]

lean_blocks.explanation: A second `variable` line, scoped to the `Walk` sub-namespace, that auto-binds the ambient CDMG `G : CDMG Node` as an implicit argument to every subsequent declaration. Both `IsSigmaOpenGiven` and `IsSigmaBlockedGiven` take a walk `p : Walk G u v` whose graph parameter must come from somewhere; without this binder each signature would have to spell out `{G : CDMG Node}` explicitly. Lifting it once at the namespace top mirrors the LN's once-at-the-top quantifier "let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG" and keeps the bodies focused on the walk and the conditioning set. `G` is kept implicit because downstream callers reach into it via dot-notation on the walk (`p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C`) — Lean recovers `G` from the type of `p`, so the user never types it. The two main predicates also call `G.AncSet C` on the bound `G`, so it must be in scope when their bodies type-check.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Namespace-wide implicit `G : CDMG Node`; recovered from the walk's type via dot-notation at every use site.
variable {G : CDMG Node}

lean_blocks.code:
def IsSigmaOpenGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  (∀ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk → p.IsCollider k →
      vk ∈ G.AncSet C) ∧
  (∀ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk →
      p.IsBlockableNonCollider k → vk ∉ C)
lean_blocks.explanation: This block defines the LN's first classifier — a walk $\pi$ is *$C$-$\sigma$-open* — as a `Prop`-valued predicate on `(p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node)`. The body is a conjunction of two parallel universals, one per LN clause. Each universal ranges over position indices `k : ℕ` together with the vertex `vk : Node` at that position, pinned down by the hypothesis `p.vertices[k]? = some vk`; this is the standard `getElem?` lookup that returns `none` past the end of the walk, so the LN's index range $k \in \{0, \dots, n\}$ is implicit — out-of-range `k` falsify the antecedent and make the implication vacuous. Clause (i) says every collider position has its vertex inside the ancestor set $\Anc^G(C)$, written here as `G.AncSet C` (the indexed-union form from `def_3_5`). Clause (ii) says every *blockable* non-collider position has its vertex outside `C`; using `p.IsBlockableNonCollider k` rather than the broader `p.IsNonCollider k` is load-bearing — unblockable non-collider positions are silently outside both clauses, matching the LN's intent. Dot-notation `p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C` reads as the LN's "$\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open".
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- ref: def_3_17 (main, LN paragraph "C-σ-open walk") — walk-level `Prop` predicate matching the LN's two-clause universal.
def IsSigmaOpenGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  -- LN clause (i): universal over `(k, vk)` pairs; `getElem?` lookup implicitly bounds `k ≤ p.length`.
  (∀ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk → p.IsCollider k →
      -- Collider positions must have their vertex inside the indexed-union ancestor set `Anc^G(C)`.
      vk ∈ G.AncSet C) ∧
  -- LN clause (ii): same `(k, vk)` shape, restricted to *blockable* non-colliders only (unblockable positions silently excluded).
  (∀ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk →
      -- Blockable non-collider positions must have their vertex outside the conditioning set `C`.
      p.IsBlockableNonCollider k → vk ∉ C)

lean_blocks.code:
def IsSigmaBlockedGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  (∃ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node),
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk ∧ p.IsCollider k ∧ vk ∉ G.AncSet C) ∨
  (∃ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node),
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk ∧ p.IsBlockableNonCollider k ∧ vk ∈ C)
lean_blocks.explanation: This block defines the LN's second classifier — a walk $\pi$ is *$C$-$\sigma$-blocked* — as the positive existential disjunction dual to `IsSigmaOpenGiven`, not as `¬ p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C`. The body is a `∨` of two existentials, each pinning a position index `k : ℕ` and the vertex `vk` at that position via `p.vertices[k]? = some vk`. Disjunct (i) is a collider witness: some position `k` is a collider on `p` whose vertex `vk` escapes `G.AncSet C`. Disjunct (ii) is a blockable-non-collider witness: some position `k` is a blockable non-collider on `p` whose vertex `vk` lies in `C`. Encoding the predicate in this positive form lets downstream proofs build a blocking witness directly as `Or.inl ⟨k, vk, h_lookup, h_collider, h_anc⟩` or `Or.inr ⟨k, vk, h_lookup, h_blockable, h_mem⟩`, instead of pushing a double negation through nested universals. The classical De Morgan equivalence `¬ p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C ↔ p.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C` is left as a downstream lemma rather than a definitional reduction.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- ref: def_3_17 (main, LN paragraph "C-σ-blocked walk") — positive existential disjunction, NOT `¬ IsSigmaOpenGiven`.
def IsSigmaBlockedGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  -- LN clause (i) dual: a collider witness whose vertex escapes the ancestor set `Anc^G(C)`.
  (∃ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node),
      -- Triple-conjunct destructures cleanly as `⟨h_lookup, h_collider, h_anc⟩` at the use site.
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk ∧ p.IsCollider k ∧ vk ∉ G.AncSet C) ∨
  -- LN clause (ii) dual: a *blockable* non-collider witness whose vertex lies in `C` (unblockable positions cannot witness).
  (∃ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node),
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk ∧ p.IsBlockableNonCollider k ∧ vk ∈ C)

addition_to_the_LN: [claim_type_mismatch_vertex_vs_walk] The trailing claim asserting that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open is excluded from the formalization. It is informal commentary, not part of Definition~\ref{def:sigma_blocking}, and no Lean obligation should be derived from it.
design_choices:
**Walk-level `Prop` predicates over `(p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node)`.** The LN classifies *walks* as $\sigma$-open or $\sigma$-blocked relative to a conditioning set, so `Walk.IsSigmaOpenGiven` and `Walk.IsSigmaBlockedGiven` take the walk and the set directly and dot-notation `p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C` reads as the LN's "$\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open". No vertex-level $\sigma$-open predicate is introduced: the trailing LN remark that "unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open" applies the walk-level predicate to vertices, which the addition `[claim_type_mismatch_vertex_vs_walk]` explicitly records as a type mismatch and excludes from this row. Any walk-level reformulation of its underlying intent is deferred to `claim_3_21`.

**Reuse `p.IsCollider` and `p.IsBlockableNonCollider` verbatim from `def_3_15` and `def_3_16`.** Collider and blockable-non-collider positions are already classified by the prior two rows; using those predicates as antecedents keeps the LN's "for every collider on $\pi$" / "for every blockable non-collider on $\pi$" scope visible at the type level, instead of re-spelling the arrowhead-count or outgoing-walk-edge case-splits. An alternative that ranges over the existential witnesses already inside `IsCollider` (e.g. `∀ k a₁ a₂, p.edges[k-1]? = some a₁ → …`) would inline the collider definition and break the one-to-one LN-to-Lean correspondence.

**Position–vertex coupling via `p.vertices[k]? = some vk`.** The LN writes $v_k$ for the vertex at position $k$; the option-valued lookup pins `vk` to the walk and implicitly bounds $k \le n$, since out-of-range lookups are `none`. The universal antecedents of `IsSigmaOpenGiven` then become vacuous on out-of-range positions, and the existential witnesses of `IsSigmaBlockedGiven` cannot be formed — matching the LN's scoping to $\{0, \dots, n\}$ without an explicit upper-bound hypothesis. This mirrors the idiom already used in `def_3_15` and `def_3_16`.

**`p.IsBlockableNonCollider k`, not `p.IsNonCollider k`.** Clause (ii) of the LN restricts to *blockable* non-colliders specifically: unblockable positions are silently outside both classifications — "vacuously open" in the walk-level sense. The broader `IsNonCollider` would over-fire on the open side (forcing unblockable positions outside $C$) and under-fire on the blocked side (admitting unblockable positions in $C$ as blocking witnesses), neither of which the LN says. The restriction is the predicate-level realisation of the LN's intent and is the structural counterpart of the `[claim_type_mismatch_vertex_vs_walk]` exclusion at the def site.

**Positive existential disjunction for `IsSigmaBlockedGiven`, not `¬ IsSigmaOpenGiven`.** The LN spells $C$-$\sigma$-blocked as $\exists \dots \notin \Anc^G(C) \;\lor\; \exists \dots \in C$, the De Morgan dual of the open definition. Encoding it directly in that positive existential form mirrors the LN's literal writing, lets downstream proofs build a blocking witness as an immediate `Or.inl ⟨k, vk, h_lookup, h_collider, h_anc⟩` or `Or.inr ⟨k, vk, h_lookup, h_blockable, h_mem⟩` term, and avoids pushing a double negation through nested universals and conjunctions every time. The triple-conjunct inside each existential destructures cleanly as `⟨h_lookup, h_predicate, h_membership⟩` at the use site. The classical equivalence `¬ p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C ↔ p.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C` is a downstream De Morgan lemma to be proved when a row needs it, not a definitional reduction the def-shape forces; encoding blockedness as the negation of openness was considered and rejected because it would invert the readability win on every witness-construction proof.

**Parallel two-clause shape on both predicates, not a single quantifier over a combined predicate.** The LN spells each classification as two parallel clauses; matching that shape lets `IsSigmaOpenGiven` destructure as `⟨h_collider, h_blockable⟩` and keeps the existential witnesses of `IsSigmaBlockedGiven` symmetric to those clauses, so any future De Morgan duality lemma aligns witness-to-witness. A single universal over a sum-typed predicate would compile but force every downstream proof to recover the bullet-list shape by hand.

**Reuse `G.AncSet` from `def_3_5` for $\Anc^G(C)$.** `CDMG.AncSet : CDMG Node → Set Node → Set Node` already encodes the indexed-union ancestor set $\bigcup_{c \in C} \Anc^G(c)$. Out-of-graph elements of $C$ contribute `G.Anc c = ∅` via the `w ∈ G` guard inside `Anc`, so the LN's $C \subseteq J \cup V$ hypothesis is not needed at the def site — an escaping $C$ simply restricts the effective ancestor set to $\Anc^G(C \cap (J \cup V))$. Downstream consumers that genuinely need the subset hypothesis pass it at the use site, following the chapter convention from `def_3_5`'s `PaSet` / `AncSet` / `DescSet`.

**Namespace-level `variable` binders for `Node`, `[DecidableEq Node]`, and `G : CDMG Node`.** Both predicates take a walk `p : Walk G u v` and reach into `G` for `G.AncSet`; lifting `{Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]` at the `CDMG` namespace and `{G : CDMG Node}` at the inner `Walk` namespace matches the chapter convention and the LN's once-at-the-top "Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG" quantifier. Decidability is inherited because `G.AncSet`, `p.IsCollider`, and `p.IsBlockableNonCollider` unfold through `Finset.filter`, `Finset.image`, and option-valued list lookups on `p.vertices`. `G` is kept implicit because callers recover it from the walk's type via dot-notation.
